title: CommBank readies ‘Can’ brand evolution with new agency appointment
full_text:
Commonwealth Bank aims to evolve its longrunning ‘Can’ positioning as it resets its brand and marketing following the appointment of a new advertising agency Howatson+Company.
The country’s biggest bank wants to build on its leadership position and evolve “how it connects with customers in a rapidly changing technology and media landscape” with the new agency working alongside CommBank’s growing in-house agency team to drive brand strategy and creative direction.
It comes as CommBank continues to evolve its services for customers, including its media and content products such as its network of 2000 digital screens and the Brighter content platform, which creates finance and provides consumers with money management content for consumers.
The new agency appointment aims to help the brand maintain its market leadership position and ensure it is “future-ready”, according to CommBank chief marketing officer Jo Boundy.
“CommBank has a strong legacy of creativity and brand leadership, and we’re focused on continuing to advance how we show up for our customers across every interaction and channel,” said Ms Boundy. “As technology and customer behaviours continue to change, we’re building a modern creative model that combines strategic thinking, creativity, speed and innovation to support our future ambitions.” The move sees CommBank part ways with its long standing agency M+C Saatchi after working together for 14 years in a relationship Ms Boundy called “deeply valued”.
M+C Saatchi is credited with creating “some of the most recognised and effective brand work in the category” including the bank’s ‘Can’ tagline, which Ms Boundy said was “one of Australia’s strongest brand platforms.”
The CommBank brand is recognised as one of the strongest marketing platforms in the country, and the strongest within the banking and financial services category. It is ranked as Australia’s most valuable brand by Kantar’s BrandZ rankings and it is also the only Australian brand to rank in the most valuable global brands list, where it jumped sixth places this year to rank 68th overall.
Kantar Australia head of brand strategy Ryan France said CommBank’s strong performance was a reflection of the brand’s customer focus, which he described as “more human, less institutional, and connected to modern Australian life, all wrapped in distinctive and consistent brand codes.”
The new agency will commence work on the brand’s marketing in the first half of 2027.
